title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time | Snurblog — Axel Bruns
snippet: Liveblog coverage of the 2026 International Communication Association (ICA) conference in Cape Town, Cape Town. The post features an in-depth summary of Fabio Giglietto's co-authored paper exploring the relationship between political partisanship, journalistic quality, and algorithmic amplification on Facebook over six years.
description: A detailed blog summary of Fabio Giglietto's co-authored paper on Facebook's algorithmic amplification and news engagement, presented at the 2026 ICA conference.

title: Indagine urbinate: "Così l’algoritmo influenza il voto" - Il Resto del Carlino

title: Algoritmi e democrazia, il caso Meta secondo lo studio di UniUrb
snippet: Press coverage detailing how Fabio Giglietto's research at the University of Urbino Carlo Bo on algorithmic amplification was featured on prime-time television and triggered European Union actions. Giglietto is quoted explaining the asymmetric, democratic deficit caused by platform governance decisions that went largely unnoticed before being uncovered using Digital Services Act transparency tools.
description: An article discussing the political and public fallout of Professor Giglietto's research on Meta's algorithms, including its feature on Rai3's 'Report' and subsequent European Parliament inquiries.

title: L'articolo 21 nella gabbia di Facebook
snippet: A journalistic analysis discussing Fabio Giglietto’s computational social science research into Facebook's visibility drop for official Italian politicians. The coverage highlights the severe implications Giglietto's findings have on digital freedom of speech, pluralism, and constitutional rights in Italy.
description: A journalistic analysis discussing Fabio Giglietto's computational social science research on Facebook algorithmic visibility drops for Italian parliamentarians and its implications for digital freedom of speech.

title: Facebook premia gli estremisti: la ricerca dell'Università di Urbino - Lumsanews
Meno contenuti politici nei feed, la stretta di Meta è stata meno efficace contro gli estremisti - Il Sole 24 ORE

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme
snippet: An article detailing a recent research study co-authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto [2.3.6]. The study reveals how Meta's algorithmic filtering systematically suppresses the reach of official political figures in Italy (by an average of 72%), while inadvertently giving disproportionate visibility and engagement to extremist and anti-EU accounts. This coverage discusses subsequent bipartisan legislative initiatives drafted to curb this algorithmic imbalance.
description: A news article discussing a study co-authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto from the University of Urbino on how Meta's algorithms impact political and extremist content reach on Facebook.
